description.en: One of the most popular leafy vegetables in Sri Lanka alongside gotukola, mukunuwenna, and kankun. Fast-growing C4 crop with very short 35–45-day cycle, low space requirement — ideal for intensive home gardens, urban/vertical gardens, and soil-less media. The **HORDI Thampala variety** (released 2013) has light green leaves, yield > 25 t/ha, high antioxidant and nutritional content, 35–40 days to first harvest, and is recommended island-wide. As forage, crude protein is 1.5–2× that of Guinea grass at 50 days.

duration_days.quote: A new Amaranthus variety called HORDI thampala was released in 2013. This variety features light green leaves, yields greater than 25 t/ha, has high antioxidant and nutritional properties, can be harvested 35-40 days after planting
